items.title: Firmus outlines plans for 'very large' AI factory at River Murray town

children.content: An AI company says its proposed South Australia projects have the potential to create hundreds of jobs, but residents of one River Murray town say they want to know more about the potential impacts.

gallery.altText: A lot in a residential area with a big building on it.

thumbnail.altText: A lot in a residential area with a big building on it.

items.title: Hopes bones in underwater 'time capsules' will unlock more prehistory

children.content: Scientists who have been finding fossils in SA's underwater caves are hoping they can give a window into environmental changes.

image.altText: An animal bone at the bottom of sinkhole with a diver in a black wetsuit and holding a torch.

items.title: Images released to identify victims in alleged Sydney child abuser probe

children.content: Images of distinctive clothing have been released as authorities attempt to identify victims of a Sydney childcare worker who allegedly abused more than 130 children.

image.altText: Cloise up of man grinning in a green shirt, plant behind him

items.title: Researchers develop laser technology to test drinks for methanol

children.content: Adelaide University researchers have developed a way to use lasers to test closed alcohol bottles for methanol, which kills hundreds of people every year.

gallery.altText: A man with glasses wearing a grey jumper poses next to a glass bottle with brown fluid in it

thumbnail.altText: A man with glasses wearing a grey jumper poses next to a glass bottle with brown fluid in it

avatar.alt: A headshot of a smiling woman with a dark background

items.title: Australia's wine grape crush plummets to lowest in more than 25 years

children.content: Australian wine grape production has fallen to its lowest amount in more than 25 years as the industry adjusts to a combination of depressed market conditions.

image.altText: A farmer in green shirt leans on the end of a winegrape vineyard holding his hat

items.title: Coronial inquest recommends SA Police improve suicide training

children.content: The coronial inquest into the death of William "Bill" Stockman did not confirm whether his death was preventable, but made multiple recommendations to the SA police minister.

image.altText: A man with dark hair and a blue shirt sitting in front of a white background

items.title: Death of man experiencing homelessness in rural town prompts call for help

children.content: Millicent residents say they are concerned for the welfare of dozens of people sleeping rough in town.

image.altText: A man in a grey jumper in a building with graffiti on the wall

items.title: Parklands chop won't stop despite petition triggering parliamentary inquiry

children.content: Acting Premier Kyam Maher says works for the golf course redevelopment and MotoGP will continue despite a petition attracting 13,500 signatures.

image.altText: Hundreds of protesters gather on the steps of South Australia's parliament.

items.title: Crows defender Josh Worrell to face court over crash

children.content: Adelaide Crows defender Josh Worrell has been reported by police and will face court over a traffic crash last week.

image.altText: crows players Josh worrell, text walker and others walking off field

items.title: Monet, Renoir, van Gogh masterpieces appear in two new blockbuster exhibitions

children.content: More than 150 years after the first Impressionist exhibition in Paris, the Art Gallery of South Australia and Geelong Gallery cast new light on the influential art movement.

image.altText: Three paintings hung in a gallery with red walls.

items.title: Flight disruptions at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog

children.content: A number of flights have been delayed for take-off at Adelaide Airport due to heavy fog while others were either cancelled or diverted elsewhere.

image.altText: Three Qantas flights on tarmac at Adelaide Airport with fog around.

items.title: Fertiliser company fined after hundreds of complaints about manure stench

children.content: An Adelaide Hills business that piled chicken manure, coffee grounds and other waste up to twice the allowed height has been fined by a South Australian court after more than 700 complaints about the smell.

image.altText: Piles of fertilisers and dirt in rows on a large piece of land containing several sheds on the side

items.title: Legal action dropped against Ann Marie Smith's NDIS provider

children.content: Federal Court proceedings have been dropped against the former company responsible for caring for South Australian woman Ann Marie Smith, who died six years ago.

image.altText: A house converted into an office

items.title: ASX rises as SpaceX shares see $1 trillion wiped

children.content: The Aussie share market opens up as SpaceX shares see more than $1 trillion wiped. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: SpaceX founder Elon Musk speaks at a press conference

items.title: Why Australians shouldn't panic about fuel prices as war in Iran escalates again

children.content: With the price of oil rising and the fuel excise cut due to end, drivers are facing twin threats when it comes to the price of fuel, but experts say Australia is in a much better position compared with when the Middle East conflict began.

articles.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iranian targets 'threatening' Strait shipping

children.content: US Central Command says it launched a 90-minute attack targeting Iran's strategic Greater Tunb Island.

image.altText: Black and white aerial view of explosion.

articles.title: Track the latest petrol and diesel prices around Australia

children.content: See what's happening with petrol and diesel prices near you, updated constantly.

items.title: Iran frees US citizen after almost two years of imprisonment

children.content: Iran has freed a US-Iranian citizen detained since December, 2024 in what Trump says was a 'gesture of goodwill'.

image.altText: People walk near an anti-U.S. mural in Tehran, Iran, July 15, 2026.

items.title: Accused gangster to call on AFP in controversial deportation case

children.content: Lawyers for accused underworld figure Masood Zakaria want to call AFP Commissioner Krissy Barrett to give evidence as he seeks to have his murder charges thrown out of court.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a navy police officer uniform.

items.title: PM defends decision not to attend Garma Festival

children.content: Anthony Albanese defends his decision to break a promise and not to attend the Garma Festival this year. Follow live.

image.altText: a man wearing an akubra walking among aboriginal dancers waving red flags

items.title: 'My father was an antisemite': Uni leader's personal submission to royal commission

children.content: The chancellor of Western Sydney University says tertiary institutions have hidden behind freedom of expression to avoid taking action on hate speech.

image.altText: Jennifer Westacott against a dark background

items.title: Liberal HQ says young voters don't remember Howard in 'reality check' to base

children.content: Liberal Party HQ has admitted it is "in the political fight of our lives" as it canvasses ideas, including reviving gender quotas, to take the party forward.

image.altText: A man in a blue suit stares at the camera, with two people standing behind him

items.title: Country footballer handed life ban after opposition complaint

children.content: A player and assistant coach for a southern NSW AFL team has been handed a "life ban" following a tribunal hearing into eight offences.

image.altText: A player in an AFL guernsey doing a ridgy didge hand signal

items.title: Too many cooks spoil The Odyssey's bubbling epic

children.content: The scope of Christopher Nolan's latest epic might be awesome but a menagerie of supporting faces and questionable characterisations muddle the director's vision.

gallery.altText: A man dressed as a solider stands in front of a crowd and ships.
Robert Pattinson is one of the side characters offered something meatier as murderous suitor Antinous.

items.title: I thought I could use voodoo to win a TV competition, instead I won back my joy

children.content: Halfway through the Race Around The World competition, I'm second last on the leaderboard. So, I decide to cheat.

image.altText: A voodoo doll of Kate.

items.title: From Mozart to the Beatles, prove you're a music genius with this quiz

children.content: From famous icons and musical terms to unusual instruments and classical influences in popular music, test your knowledge with this quiz.

image.altText: Celine Dion in a black leather jacket sings into a handheld microphone under warm yellow stage lights during a concert.

items.title: Decisions made by 'faceless people' in NDIS abandoning those in need, family says

children.content: Experts say the National Disability Insurance Scheme is failing people like Lucy, whose one-on-one care for severe disability has been deemed "not value for money".

gallery.altText: A man and two women standing together for a photo.

thumbnail.altText: A man and two women standing together for a photo.

items.title: Scars of alleged Invasion Day terror attack run deep six months on

children.content: A day of evidence reflecting on racism, hate and violence directed towards Aboriginal people highlights the ongoing impact of what police described as a potential "mass-casualty event" at a Perth rally.

image.altText: About a dozen police vehicles parked across a road to close it off.

items.title: Artists seek ‘seat at table’ in AI office

children.content: The creative sector hopes there is a space to contribute to the Office of AI, given its content powers a lot of these tools.

image.altText: A woman in black sits in front of an ARIA sign.

items.title: Train drivers trapped, homes evacuated as wildfires rage across Canada's Ontario

children.content: Train operators in rural Canada were trapped by raging wildfires, as nearby communities were evacuated.

image.altText: Wildfires surrounding a train

gallery.altText: A swirling inferno seen from the inside of a freight train.

thumbnail.altText: A swirling inferno seen from the inside of a freight train.

items.title: Telstra and telco regulator prepare for grilling over widespread outage

children.content: Telstra prepares for a grilling in parliament about the recent network outage that disrupted Australia, with the communications regulator also set to face tough questions.

image.altText: A woman in a blazer holds a document and looks serious.

items.title: UK's Reform proposes round-the-clock protection for MPs

children.content: Britain's populist Reform UK, led by Nigel Farage, has called for all lawmakers to be given "full security" if they want it after the alleged murder of Ann Widdecombe, a prominent member of the party.

image.altText: Nigel Farage speaks in front of a microphone, and a blue background. He is holding his hands up as he speaks.

items.title: Disclosure scheme could have prevented intimate partner abuse, woman says

children.content: A survivor of family violence wants victims to have access to information about a person's previous offences.

gallery.altText: A woman stands silhouetted in front of a window.

thumbnail.altText: A woman stands silhouetted in front of a window.

items.title: 'I want the money that is owed to me': Charity fails to pay $150k in super

children.content: Former staff of charity PAWS Darwin are speaking out about being short-changed on their superannuation to the tune of $150,000. Under-18 staff also say the super accounts PAWS promised to create for them never existed.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a white woman, with blonde hair tied back, black singlet top, sitting among greenery.

items.title: Fastest-ever Tour de France stage sees Norwegian win in 'crazy' result

children.content: Tadej Pogačar protects his lead as Norway's Soeren Waerenskjold claims the fastest-ever stage in Tour de France history.

image.altText: Soren Waerenskjold holds one fist in the air and screams hunched forward over the bike.

items.title: Ancestral remains begin long journey home after 143 years at museum

children.content: The remains of members of the Rapa Nui people from Easter Island are "finally going home" after sitting in the Australian Museum's collection for over a century.

gallery.altText: Repatriation of Rapa Nui ancestral remains from Australian Museum
